title: All Uk Number 1 Hits
text: The UK singles chart, a weekly countdown of the most popular songs on the radio, has been a constant in our lives for seventy years. But to Dave Watson, it’s a lifestyle. Watson has been collecting copies of UK Number 1 hits since the late 1980s; today, he owns all 1,404 UK Number 1 singles, reaching back to the birth of the charts in 1952. He believes it’s the only complete collection of its kind. Watson’s devotion to music charts began more than thirty years ago. Starting a collection made sense: his mother had worked in a record store, and he enjoyed collecting things. When he started his mission in 1988, the charts had already seen 605 Number 1 singles. With the single “Don’t Turn Around” under his arm, he decided to find the previous 604 releases. In his treasure hunt, Watson used to get trains to London to visit music fairs and second-hand record shops. He also responded to selling adverts in Time Out and Loot magazines and wrote to record dealers. “I spent endless hours searching through dealers’ stock,” he says. “I had a handwritten list that I photocopied and took around with me to spread the word. Some sellers answered back saying which singles they had and how much they would be.” His first wish list was already several pages long. Then he started crossing titles out and he told himself, “Well, I have a chance of doing all this.” Discovering eBay around the millennium was a turning point. With a mouse click, Watson found there the last ten singles he needed. With the 1953 single “Doggy in the Window” Watson brought the collection up to date.
